meta_title: Impact Windows in Plantation, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Plantation, FL. Protect your home, lower insurance costs & meet Broward County code. Free estimates!
h1: Impact Windows in Plantation, FL — Whole-Home Hurricane Protection for Broward's Most Established Neighborhoods
intro:
Plantation, Florida is one of Broward County's most desirable places to call home — and for good reason. From the tree-lined streets of Jacaranda and the serene waterways of Plantation Isles to the spacious lots of Plantation Acres and the close-knit community feel of Pine Island Ridge, this city of roughly 95,000 residents has cultivated a reputation for stability, great schools, and well-maintained homes. With average home values hovering around $430,000, homeowners here have made a serious investment — and protecting that investment against South Florida's relentless hurricane season is not optional, it's essential.

Unlike beachfront communities to the east, Plantation sits approximately eight miles from the Atlantic coast, a distance that might suggest a degree of shelter. But anyone who lived here during Hurricane Wilma in 2005 or felt the wrath of Hurricane Irma in 2017 knows firsthand that inland Broward County is far from immune to catastrophic wind and storm damage. Wind gusts tear through subdivisions, flying debris shatters ordinary glass, and rain-driven infiltration causes tens of thousands of dollars in interior damage — all in a matter of hours. Plantation's 170 mph design wind speed rating means every window and door on your home must be engineered to perform under extreme pressure, and standard builder-grade windows simply don't meet that bar.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has helped hundreds of Plantation homeowners make the upgrade to certified impact-resistant windows — the kind that don't just survive a storm, but actively protect your family, reduce your energy bills, block UV damage to your furnishings, and earn you meaningful discounts on your homeowner's insurance. If your home was built before 1994 — a category that applies to roughly 45% of Plantation's housing stock — you are almost certainly living with windows that were never designed to meet modern Florida Building Code hurricane standards. That's a gap in protection worth closing, and Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is the team Plantation trusts to close it right.

section_why.h2: Why Plantation Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
Choosing an impact window contractor is one of the most consequential home improvement decisions you'll make, and the Plantation market is not short on options. What separates Premier Impact Windows & Roofing from the competition isn't a flashy sales pitch — it's a track record built across 500+ completed homes in Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, a 100% in-house installation team, and more than 60 years of combined field experience. When you call Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, the same people who show up to measure your home are the licensed, trained technicians who will install your windows. There are no subcontractors, no handoffs, and no accountability gaps.

For homeowners in neighborhoods like Central Park and Jacaranda, where home layouts often feature large picture windows, sliding glass doors, and open floor plans, precision installation matters enormously. A poorly shimmed frame, an improperly cured sealant bead, or an undertorqued fastener can compromise the structural performance of even the best impact window under high-wind conditions. Our installation crews understand the nuances of Plantation's older concrete block construction as well as newer wood-frame builds, and we pull every permit through the City of Plantation Building Division — ensuring every job is inspected and documented for your records, your insurance carrier, and future resale purposes.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing also brings a level of product knowledge that generalist contractors simply can't match. We are authorized installers for ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI — two brands specifically engineered for South Florida's demanding wind and moisture environment. Our team will walk you through which product lines, frame configurations, and glass packages make the most sense for each elevation of your home, your HOA's aesthetic requirements, and your budget. We don't believe in one-size-fits-all proposals.

And when the job is done, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing stands behind it. Our A+ BBB rating reflects years of responsive customer service, clean job sites, and installations that pass inspection the first time. We offer financing starting from just $87 per month with $0 down options, making it easier than ever for Plantation families to upgrade without waiting years to save the cash. The Kings of Service isn't just a tagline — it's a standard we hold ourselves to on every single project in Broward County.

section_history.h2: Plantation's Storm History Makes the Case for Impact Windows
section_history.body:
Plantation has weathered nine named storms since 1990, and each one has left its mark on the community in ways that residents don't soon forget. The most pivotal event in Broward County's modern history of hurricane preparedness was Hurricane Andrew in August 1992. Although Andrew made direct landfall in Miami-Dade, the storm's catastrophic destruction — particularly the wholesale failure of windows, doors, and roofs — sent shockwaves through the entire building industry and prompted Florida to completely overhaul its building code. Plantation's older neighborhoods, many of which were developed through the 1970s and 1980s, were built under standards that Andrew exposed as dangerously inadequate.

Thirteen years later, Hurricane Wilma made its own statement. Crossing the Florida peninsula in October 2005 from the southwest, Wilma caught many Broward residents off guard with its rapid intensification and its unusually broad damage footprint. In Plantation, roofs were peeled back, carport enclosures failed, and streets were littered with glass from broken windows. Damage claims across Broward County ran into the billions of dollars — much of it covered by emergency tarps and insurance claims that stretched for months. Neighborhoods like Plantation Isles and Pine Island Ridge saw extensive tree canopy damage that turned ordinary windborne debris into high-velocity projectiles.

Hurricane Irma in September 2017 served as a more recent reminder. While not as catastrophically damaging in Broward as it was farther south, Irma brought sustained winds well above 60 mph to Plantation and downed thousands of trees across the city, including near Plantation Heritage Park and along the corridors feeding into the Westfield Broward Mall area. Homeowners who had already upgraded to impact windows reported minimal damage; those who had not faced broken glass, flooded interiors, and lengthy claims processes. The pattern across all three storms is unmistakable — impact-rated protection changes outcomes in a measurable, documented way.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Compliance for Impact Windows in Plantation and Broward County
section_code.body:
Understanding Florida's building code requirements is critical before purchasing any impact window product in Plantation. The city falls within Broward County and is subject to the Florida Building Code — one of the most stringent residential construction codes in the nation, updated every three years and enforced rigorously at the local level by the City of Plantation Building Division. While Plantation is not located within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a special designation that applies primarily to Miami-Dade and Broward coastal communities — the city's 170 mph design wind speed rating still demands products that meet or exceed demanding performance thresholds.

All replacement impact windows installed in Plantation must carry a current Florida Product Approval (FPA) number, verifiable through the Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation (DBPR) product approval database. This approval certifies that the specific window unit — frame material, glass configuration, and hardware — has been independently tested and approved for the applicable wind load and impact requirements. Products that are appropriate for Miami-Dade HVHZ carry the highest certifications and are more than sufficient for Plantation's non-HVHZ requirements, which is exactly why Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's ESW and CWI product lines are well-suited for this market.

Permit requirements in Plantation are non-negotiable. Every impact window replacement project requires a permit from the City of Plantation Building Division, followed by a post-installation inspection to verify compliance. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application submission to coordinating the final inspection — so you don't have to navigate local bureaucracy on your own. Permitted installations are also essential for your insurance wind mitigation inspection, and they protect your home's resale value by ensuring future buyers can verify the work was done correctly and to code. Skipping the permit process to save time or money is a risk no reputable contractor in Plantation should take, and Premier Impact Windows & Roofing never does.

section_products.h2: ESW Eastern Storm Windows and CWI: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's Trusted Brands for Plantation Homes
section_products.body:
Not all impact windows are created equal, and in Plantation's climate — where the combination of hurricane-force wind threats, intense UV radiation, and salt-laden humidity from the nearby coast demands products built for the long haul — brand selection matters. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading product lines: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are specifically engineered for South Florida's conditions, carry the required Florida Product Approvals, and have been installed across hundreds of homes throughout Broward County.

ESW impact windows feature laminated glass constructed with a polyvinyl butyral (PVB) interlayer — the same core technology used in automotive windshields. When subjected to impact from windborne debris, ESW glass is designed to crack but not shatter, maintaining the integrity of the building envelope and preventing wind-driven rain from infiltrating your home. ESW frames incorporate multi-chamber profiles that provide superior structural rigidity under positive and negative wind pressure cycling, while integrated Low-E coatings help reject solar heat gain — a feature that makes a meaningful difference in monthly energy bills during Plantation's long, hot summers.

CWI impact windows offer Plantation homeowners another excellent option, particularly for larger architectural openings and custom configurations common in the more upscale custom-built homes found throughout Plantation Acres and the newer sections of Jacaranda. CWI products are available in a range of frame finishes and grid patterns that satisfy even strict HOA aesthetic guidelines, without sacrificing performance. Both ESW and CWI products complement each other within our product offering, allowing Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's consultants to recommend the right solution for each specific application — whether you're replacing a standard double-hung, a large fixed picture window, an awning unit, or a sliding glass door — ensuring every opening on your home meets the same high standard of protection.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Windows Cost in Plantation — And Why the Investment Makes Sense
section_cost.body:
For homeowners in Plantation, where the average property value sits at approximately $430,000, the cost of impact window installation should be viewed in the context of the asset it protects. A full-home impact window replacement project typically ranges from $12,000 to $35,000 or more depending on the number of openings, window sizes, frame material, and glass package selected. Larger homes in neighborhoods like Plantation Isles or Central Park with extensive glass square footage will naturally fall toward the higher end of that range. Smaller ranch-style homes throughout Plantation Acres may come in closer to the lower end. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides detailed, itemized quotes so you know exactly what you're getting before any agreement is signed.

Financing has made impact window upgrades far more accessible for Plantation families than many homeowners realize. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing options starting from just $87 per month with $0 down required, allowing you to begin protecting your home immediately without depleting your savings or waiting years to accumulate the full cost. For a $430,000 home, the mathematics of impact window financing are compelling: the monthly payment is often less than the increase in homeowner's insurance premium you might face after a major storm claim — or less than the incremental monthly cost of running older, inefficient windows through a South Florida summer.

The return on investment picture extends well beyond storm protection. Independent appraisers consistently find that impact windows add meaningful resale value to South Florida homes, with many studies suggesting a return of 70–80 cents on the dollar at resale — and that's before accounting for energy savings, insurance discounts, and the avoided cost of storm shutters. In a competitive Plantation real estate market, a fully permitted impact window installation is a genuine selling point that resonates with educated buyers and their inspectors alike.

section_insurance.h2: How Impact Windows in Plantation Can Significantly Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance
section_insurance.body:
Florida homeowners insurance has never been more expensive, and Broward County residents — including those in Plantation — are feeling the pressure acutely as carriers re-evaluate their exposure across the state. One of the most effective steps a Plantation homeowner can take to reduce insurance costs is completing a qualifying wind mitigation upgrade, and fully permitted impact windows are among the highest-value improvements on a wind mitigation inspector's checklist. Under the Florida Office of Insurance Regulation's wind mitigation credit framework, homes with impact-rated openings on all windows and doors can qualify for the maximum opening protection credit — a category that insurers consistently reward with substantial premium reductions.

The magnitude of the savings depends on your specific carrier, your home's construction type, and your current policy structure, but discounts on the wind portion of a homeowner's premium commonly range from 20% to 45% for homes with complete opening protection. In a market where annual homeowner's insurance premiums in Broward County can easily range from $4,000 to $8,000 or higher for a mid-value home, those percentage reductions translate into real dollars — often $800 to $2,500 or more annually. Over a ten-year period, those accumulated savings can represent a substantial offset against the original cost of your impact window investment.

After Premier Impact Windows & Roofing completes your installation and the City of Plantation Building Division signs off on the permit inspection, we strongly recommend scheduling a licensed wind mitigation inspector to update your home's wind mitigation report and submit it to your insurance carrier. Many carriers — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, and Heritage Insurance — accept these reports and adjust premiums at your next renewal cycle. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can guide you through what documentation your carrier will need, ensuring you capture every dollar of credit you've earned through your investment in impact window protection.

faqs.q: Are impact windows required in Plantation, FL, or are storm shutters an acceptable alternative?
faqs.a: Both impact windows and storm shutters can satisfy Florida Building Code requirements for opening protection in Plantation. However, impact windows offer year-round protection without the hassle of deploying shutters before each storm, and they provide additional benefits like energy efficiency, noise reduction, and insurance discounts that shutters typically do not. For most Plantation homeowners — especially those in established neighborhoods with older windows — impact windows are the more practical and cost-effective long-term solution.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost for an average home in Plantation, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: For a typical Plantation home, a full impact window replacement project commonly ranges from $12,000 to $35,000 depending on window count, sizes, and product selection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down required, making it possible to start your project immediately. Our team provides free, no-obligation in-home estimates with itemized quotes so you can make an informed decision without any pressure.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my windows with impact windows in Plantation?
faqs.a: Yes. The City of Plantation Building Division requires a permit for all impact window replacement projects, regardless of the number of windows being replaced. A post-installation inspection is also required to verify code compliance. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application to final inspection sign-off — at no additional coordination cost to you. This ensures your installation is fully documented for insurance and resale purposes.

faqs.q: How long does a typical impact window installation take for a Plantation home?
faqs.a: Most single-family home installations in Plantation are completed in one to three days by our in-house installation team, depending on the number of windows and complexity of the project. The permitting process through the City of Plantation Building Division typically adds a few weeks to the overall timeline before installation begins. Our team will give you a clear project schedule during the proposal phase and keep you informed at every stage so there are no surprises.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowner's insurance after installing impact windows in Plantation?
faqs.a: Savings vary by carrier and policy, but Broward County homeowners with complete impact-rated opening protection frequently see wind mitigation premium discounts ranging from 20% to 45% on the wind portion of their policy. With annual homeowner's insurance premiums in Plantation commonly ranging from $4,000 to $8,000 or more, that can represent $800 to $2,500 or more in annual savings. After installation, we recommend submitting an updated wind mitigation report to your carrier to begin capturing those credits as quickly as possible.
